summary: A defensive-RAG governance doctrine whose first job is to stop immature security claims from inflating themselves.
final_use_case: Its value is the language firewall: it makes words like secure, robust, solved, government-grade, and production-ready unavailable until evidence earns them.

instruments.instrument_name: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) Artifact Identity Register
instruments.input: packet name, source files, version signal, scope, and intended destination
instruments.operation: A defensive-RAG governance doctrine whose first job is to stop immature security claims from inflating themselves.
instruments.output: what the packet is and what decision it can still change
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires packet name, source files, version signal, scope, and intended destination
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds what the packet is and what decision it can still change; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: what the packet is and what decision it can still change
instruments.status: Status: Every control in the lab currently sits at the first lifecycle gate — Designed. Nothing has been promoted to Implemented, because nothing is yet.
instruments.gap: Verify one representative case: the step should accept packet name, source files, version signal, scope, and intended destination and produce what the packet is and what decision it can still change.
instruments.verification_check: Run one corpus query or source-admission fixture through Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) Artifact Identity Register; confirm source identity, retrieval path, support state, and output: what the packet is.

instruments.instrument_name: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) Mechanism Or Claim Distillation
instruments.input: the packet's central method, model, concept, or design rule
instruments.operation: Every claim must carry mission profile, attacker tier, objective class, evidence packet, current boundary, residual risk, and downgrade triggers before it can mature through C1 Designed, C2 Implemented, C3 Validated, C4 Adaptively Stress-Tested, C5 Operationally Sustainable, and C6 Externally Defensible stages, with NIST AI RMF, NIST SP, and Google SAIF-style controls as reference discipline.
instruments.output: the distinctive idea that should not disappear in public summary

instruments.instrument_name: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) Proof Boundary
instruments.input: built assets, partial assets, archive-only assets, and unsupported claims
instruments.operation: Every control in the lab currently sits at the first lifecycle gate — Designed. Nothing has been promoted to Implemented, because nothing is yet implemented; the lab has chosen to wait rather than promote on documentation alone. This is the discipline working on its own author, not a confession of weakness. The five live claims describe what the lab is right now: a first-draft governance pack, a limited model of the control plane, a model of provenance and transformation and chunk lineage, a limited set of.

instruments.instrument_name: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) Promotion Route
instruments.input: website room, MuranoOS room, Nexus task, paper, source bank, or archive
instruments.operation: The lab is not waiting to write more doctrine. It is waiting to acquire a real defensive operation to govern, and then to graduate the controls one at a time by producing the kind of evidence each gate demands. The order is fixed by what the lab is structurally waiting for. Step 01 Anti-Distillation online Row 03 prototype has to come online first — until it exists, the lab has nothing real to govern, and any promotion above C1 would be the lab inflating its own claims. Step 02 Designed — Implemented Each control.
instruments.output: where the packet should go next as a reviewable artifact
instruments.upstream_dependency: Requires website room, MuranoOS room, Nexus task, paper, source bank, or archive
instruments.downstream_use: Feeds where the packet should go next as a reviewable artifact; later claims should not rely on this step until its expected output is present.
instruments.proof: Expected proof artifact: where the packet should go next as a reviewable artifact
instruments.status: Status: Every control in the lab currently sits at the first lifecycle gate — Designed. Nothing has been promoted to Implemented, because nothing is yet.

instruments.instrument_name: Defense Doctrine — defensive retrieval governance laboratory (claim-limit discipline) / NIST AI
instruments.input: project source text and the named component `NIST AI`
instruments.operation: uses `NIST AI` as a named project component with a traceable role in the public mechanism. Source context: Anchored in NIST AI 600-1, NIST SP 800-61 Rev. 3, and Google SAIF Controls — the recognized governance vocabulary for AI security work — but extended with the harder rule that a claim which cannot survive narrower wording is not yet mature. Every claim made inside the lab — about controls, about defenses, about results — has to pass through six gates in order.
